Model comparison (macro level)¶

Room popularity, Edge-load. Real tracks vs simulated (Unity).

Input: DXF with floor plan and zones, folder with real-trajectory CSVs, Simulation layer.

Simulation parameters (Unity)¶

  • 51 agents, start interval 2-6 s, 4 types: active explorer 15%, fast 30%, researcher 30%, slow 25%
In [45]:
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# So that room_popularity import works when run from model_search or repo root
for d in [Path.cwd(), Path.cwd() / "model_search"]:
    if (d / "room_popularity.py").exists() and str(d) not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, str(d))
        break

# DXF: in this folder or model_search/ when run from root
PATH_DXF = str(Path("model_search") / "Floor_0.dxf") if (Path.cwd() / "model_search" / "Floor_0.dxf").exists() else "Floor_0.dxf"
PATH_TRAJECTORIES_CSV = "C://Users/malko/Desktop/Diploma/Term/bird-dataset-main/bird-dataset-main/data/normalized_trajectories"
LAYER_FLOOR_PLAN = "Floor_plan"
LAYER_AREA = "Area"
PATH_UNITY_DXF = str(Path("model_search") / "unity_plan_and_tracks.dxf") if (Path.cwd() / "model_search" / "unity_plan_and_tracks.dxf").exists() else "unity_plan_and_tracks.dxf"
LAYER_UNITY_TRACKS = "TRACKS"  # simulation tracks in unity DXF

from macro_compare import run_room_popularity_comparison, run_edge_load_comparison, run_global_summary_macro
from macro_data import load_tracks_and_plot, load_edge_load_real, load_edge_load_sim
In [46]:
%pip install -q ezdxf pandas shapely matplotlib scipy

Note: you may need to restart the kernel to use updated packages.

[notice] A new release of pip is available: 26.0 -> 26.0.1
[notice] To update, run: python.exe -m pip install --upgrade pip

Tracks: real and simulated (with floor plan)¶

Below: two visualizations — real tracks from the dataset and simulated tracks from Unity. Both overlaid on the DXF floor plan.

In [47]:
plan_segments, traj_real, traj_sim = load_tracks_and_plot(
    PATH_DXF, LAYER_FLOOR_PLAN, PATH_TRAJECTORIES_CSV, PATH_UNITY_DXF, LAYER_UNITY_TRACKS
)

Room popularity ranking (real)¶

Count: how many unique agents visited each zone. Chain from least to most popular (zone | n_agents_visited | rank).

In [48]:
import importlib
import room_popularity
importlib.reload(room_popularity)
from room_popularity import compute_room_popularity_ranking, load_trajectories_from_csv

# Room popularity (real) — tracks from CSV
traj_real = load_trajectories_from_csv(PATH_TRAJECTORIES_CSV, floor_number=0)
df_real, chain_real, n_traj_real = compute_room_popularity_ranking(
    PATH_DXF, LAYER_AREA, trajectories=traj_real
)
print("Room popularity (real), n_trajectories:", n_traj_real)
display(df_real)
print("Chain:", ", ".join(map(str, chain_real)))

In [49]:
from room_popularity import save_ranking

out_csv, out_json = save_ranking(
    df_real, chain_real, PATH_DXF, n_traj_real,
    layer_floor_plan=LAYER_FLOOR_PLAN,
    layer_area=LAYER_AREA,
    layer_trajectories="csv",
)
print(f"Saved: {out_csv}, {out_json}")

Saved: C:\Users\malko\Desktop\Diploma\Term\bird-dataset-main\model_search\room_popularity_ranking.csv, C:\Users\malko\Desktop\Diploma\Term\bird-dataset-main\model_search\room_popularity_ranking.json

Room popularity ranking (simulated)¶

Same for simulated tracks (Simulation layer). Comparison with real shows how well simulation matches zone popularity.

In [50]:
df_sim, chain_sim, n_traj_sim = compute_room_popularity_ranking(
    PATH_DXF, LAYER_AREA, trajectories=traj_sim
)
if n_traj_sim == 0:
    print("No simulated tracks (unity DXF layer TRACKS empty or file missing).")
else:
    print("Room popularity (simulated), n_trajectories:", n_traj_sim)
    display(df_sim)
    print("Chain:", ", ".join(map(str, chain_sim)))

Room popularity comparison¶

Scatter plot (n_agents real vs sim), basic stats (Pearson and Spearman correlation), slopegraph (connects same zones between real and sim rankings).

In [51]:
run_room_popularity_comparison(df_real, df_sim, n_traj_sim)

n_real: mean=43.2, std=8.9
n_sim:  mean=37.9, std=15.8
Pearson correlation (n_agents): r=0.765, p=0.0009
Spearman correlation (rank):     r=0.907, p=0.0000

Edge-load (real)¶

Transition popularity between zones: for each (from_zone, to_zone) — share of transitions (dependency_pct). Shows which passages agents use most.

In [52]:
polygons_with_zone, zone_labels, df_trans_real, total_real = load_edge_load_real(
    PATH_DXF, LAYER_AREA, PATH_TRAJECTORIES_CSV
)

Edge-load (simulated)¶

Same for simulated tracks. Transition pattern in simulation should resemble real.

In [53]:
df_trans_sim, total_sim = load_edge_load_sim(
    PATH_DXF, PATH_UNITY_DXF, LAYER_UNITY_TRACKS, polygons_with_zone, zone_labels
)

Edge-load comparison¶

Scatter: dependency_pct real vs simulated by transition. Closeness to y=x diagonal means similar pattern.

In [54]:
# Fallback: load simulation from DXF if not already run
try:
    from room_popularity import load_simulated_trajectories_from_unity_dxf, parse_zones_from_dxf, compute_transition_matrix
except ImportError:
    pass
if "traj_sim" not in globals() or len(traj_sim) == 0:
    traj_sim = load_simulated_trajectories_from_unity_dxf(PATH_DXF, PATH_UNITY_DXF, layer_reference_bird="Outline", layer_tracks_unity=LAYER_UNITY_TRACKS)
if "polygons_with_zone" not in globals() or "zone_labels" not in globals():
    polygons_with_zone, zone_labels = parse_zones_from_dxf(PATH_DXF, LAYER_AREA)
if len(traj_sim) > 0 and ("df_trans_sim" not in globals() or "total_sim" not in globals()):
    df_trans_sim, total_sim = compute_transition_matrix(polygons_with_zone, zone_labels, traj_sim)

have_sim = len(traj_sim) > 0 and "total_sim" in globals() and total_sim > 0
if have_sim:
    run_edge_load_comparison(df_trans_real, df_trans_sim)
else:
    print("No simulated tracks for edge-load comparison.")

--- Basic edge-load stats ---
pct_real:  mean=1.5148, std=1.2338, min=0.0000, max=4.3400
pct_sim:   mean=1.5155, std=1.7591, min=0.0000, max=5.7300
count_real: mean=18.14, std=14.76, min=0, max=52
count_sim:  mean=26.17, std=30.38, min=0, max=99
--- Edge-load correlation and comparison metrics ---
dependency_pct: Pearson=0.8116, Spearman=0.8300
dependency_pct: MAE=0.7906, RMSE=1.0458
count: Pearson=0.8111, Spearman=0.8300
count: MAE=15.09

Global summary (macro)¶

Summary of real vs simulated comparison for Room popularity and Edge-load, with targets and recommendations.

In [55]:
# Global summary (macro): room popularity + edge-load (run after all comparison cells above)
from IPython.display import display, Markdown
display(Markdown(run_global_summary_macro(df_real, df_sim, n_traj_sim, df_trans_real, df_trans_sim)))

Global summary: real vs simulated (macro)¶

Room popularity¶

  • Pearson (n_agents): r = 0.765 — linear agreement on visit counts per zone.
  • Spearman (rank): rho = 0.907 — strong agreement on zone ranking.
  • Target: Spearman (rank) ≥ 0.7 ✓.

Edge-load¶

  • dependency_pct: Pearson r = 0.812, Spearman rho = 0.830 — strong agreement.
  • count: Pearson r = 0.811, Spearman rho = 0.830. MAE(pct) = 0.791.
  • Target: Spearman (dependency_pct) ≥ 0.7 ✓.

Recommendations (targets to aim for)¶

  • Room popularity: Spearman rank correlation ≥ 0.7 indicates good agreement on which zones are most/least visited. Pearson on n_agents reflects similarity in absolute counts.
  • Edge-load: Spearman ≥ 0.7 on dependency_pct (or count) indicates that transition flows between zones match well. Lower MAE on dependency_pct is better.

Summary generated from metrics computed in the cells above.
